_meta.purpose: mineral safety 统一校准表（How-to 框架 v2 §4.3）— 文章 + cleansing-timer 工具单源调用，不临场编
_meta.calibration_principle: 措辞经矿物学校准，避免'瞬间溶解/强毒性'等夸张表述伤可信度（v2 §4.3）

value_legend.brief: 短暂接触安全（快冲/快擦，不可浸泡）
value_legend.indirect: 仅间接（不可直射/长时间）

tier_legend.tier1_safe: moonlight / selenite / sound — 全石安全通用
tier_legend.tier2_conditional: smoke(通风) / water(查耐受) — 有条件

selenite.name: Selenite
selenite.notes: A soft form of gypsum (Mohs 2); can be damaged, scratched, or degraded by prolonged water exposure — keep dry. Self-cleansing in tradition; commonly used as a plate to rest other stones on.

malachite.name: Malachite
malachite.notes: Porous and copper-bearing; commonly kept away from water, salt, and acids in stone-care practice to avoid surface dulling and potential copper compound release.

amethyst.name: Amethyst
amethyst.notes: Color may fade with prolonged direct sunlight; indirect light or moonlight is the safer choice for color-sensitive stones. Durable (Mohs 7) but a color-sensitive quartz.
amethyst.source: GIA / mindat — quartz color stability reference

rose-quartz.name: Rose Quartz
rose-quartz.notes: Color may fade with prolonged direct sunlight (titanium/manganese + irradiation color cause); indirect light or moonlight is the safer choice for color-sensitive stones.
rose-quartz.source: GIA / mindat — quartz color stability reference

citrine.name: Citrine
citrine.notes: Durable stone (Mohs 7) tolerates short indirect sunlight; avoid prolonged direct sun even for durable stones to prevent heat stress. Color (iron + heat) is more stable than amethyst but prolonged sun still not advised.

clear-quartz.name: Clear Quartz
clear-quartz.notes: Durable stone (Mohs 7) tolerates short indirect sunlight; avoid prolonged direct sun even for durable stones to prevent heat stress. Brief water rinse safe; avoid prolonged soaking.

tiger-eye.name: Tiger's Eye
tiger-eye.notes: Durable stone (Mohs 7); tolerates short indirect sunlight. Brief water rinse safe. Avoid prolonged soaking and direct sun to preserve the chatoyant sheen.

black-tourmaline.name: Black Tourmaline (Schorl)
black-tourmaline.notes: Durable stone (Mohs 7–7.5), sun-stable. Brief water rinse safe. Popular daily-wear stone (bracelets) — frequent contact with skin oils/sweat makes regular cleansing practical care, not just ritual.

carnelian.name: Carnelian
carnelian.notes: Durable stone (Mohs 7), color-stable (iron oxide). Brief water rinse safe; avoid long salt soaks.

calcite.name: Calcite
calcite.notes: Soft (Mohs 3) and reacts with acids (including mild ones). Keep dry; avoid water, salt, and acidic cleaners. Use no-contact methods (moonlight / selenite / sound).

moonstone.name: Moonstone (Feldspar)
moonstone.notes: Mohs 6–6.5, two perfect cleavages — avoid rough handling and prolonged water/soaking. Indirect light preferred to protect the adularescent sheen.

fluorite.name: Fluorite
fluorite.notes: Soft (Mohs 4) and color may fade with prolonged direct sunlight. Brief water rinse safe; avoid prolonged soaking. No-contact methods preferred for specimen-quality pieces.

pyrite.name: Pyrite
pyrite.notes: Can oxidize (tarnish to rusty/brown) with prolonged moisture exposure; keep dry. Avoid water and salt. No-contact methods preferred.

hematite.name: Hematite
hematite.notes: Durable (Mohs 5.5–6.5) but can rust in prolonged water/salt. Brief rinse safe; avoid soaking. Often set in metal jewelry — dry methods preferred to protect settings.
hematite.source: GIA / mindat — iron oxide mineralogy reference

moonlight.specific_risk: Extremely low UV intensity vs direct sun (roughly 1/400,000); generally one of the safest light methods for all minerals.
moonlight.practical_caveat: Check weather before placing on a windowsill — unexpected rain can wet selenite, calcite, pyrite and other water-sensitive stones.

selenite_plate.specific_risk: No chemical interaction — completely harmless to other stones. The selenite itself is soft (Mohs 2); avoid dragging hard stones across its surface to prevent scratching.
selenite_plate.practical_caveat: Best no-contact method for jewelry — bracelet/necklace rests on the plate, no water/salt/handling involved.

sound.specific_risk: Sound waves are far too low-energy to affect any mineral's structure — harmless to every stone.
sound.practical_caveat: Fastest no-contact method (5–10 min); works for an entire collection at once.

smoke.specific_risk: Smoke will not damage stones; prolonged exposure may leave faint residue — wipe with a dry cloth. Ventilate well; keep smoke, embers, and hot ash away from children, pets, and respiratory-sensitive people. Confirm local rules (some apartments/rentals ban indoor burning); use a fire-safe vessel (abalone shell / ceramic bowl) and fully extinguish after use.
smoke.practical_caveat: Eastern alternative: a Tibetan singing bowl gives a similar ritual feel without smoke (the shop carries copper singing bowls).

water.specific_risk: Only water-tolerant stones (Mohs 6+, non-porous, non-copper-bearing). Dissolves or damages soft/water-reactive minerals (selenite, calcite, malachite, pyrite). Avoid soaking elastic-cord bracelets (cord degrades) and prolonged wetting of metal chains (oxidation).
water.practical_caveat: Cool running water, brief rinse (~1 min), dry immediately with a soft cloth.

earth.specific_risk: Only high-hardness stones (Mohs 7+); soil moisture and organic acids can stain or etch soft, porous stones — always wrap in natural cloth first. No metal settings (soil moisture tarnishes/loosens them).
earth.practical_caveat: A deep grounding ritual — bury in a garden or houseplant pot for ~24 hours; brush off soil gently after.

salt.specific_risk: The most damaging method — abrasive, corrodes metals (silver/copper chains oxidize faster), etches soft stones. Not ideal for metal-chain jewelry or porous stones. Dry salt only, never saltwater (saltwater is not recommended for any specimen-quality crystal).
salt.practical_caveat: Only Mohs 7+ raw stones, a few hours maximum; discard salt afterward (do not reuse).
salt.source: mineralogy reference — salt abrasiveness + metal corrosion

safety_quick_reference_table.note: Dry / no-contact methods only — moonlight, selenite plate, sound, smoke
No direct sun (color may fade) — moonlight is the safer light method
Durable but avoid prolonged soaking; for jewelry, selenite/moonlight still the gentlest default
The four universal-safe methods — use these when unsure of a stone's tolerance
